image.alt: Portrait of Yatong holding a big fuwa Miku at Anime Expo 2025

bio.tagline: Brewing designs with a blend of what, why, & how thru a humanist lens.
paragraphs: For most of my school years, I studied East Asian Studies with a focus on Japanese culture. I found myself trying to figure out "what" and "why", but rarely proposing "how".
I wanted to move beyond analysis and get my hands dirty. To make and create. To answer the "how".

It may sounds cliché, but reading Don Norman's book 『 The Design of Everyday Things 』 gave me an Eureka moment. I was fascinated by how our life experiences are shaped by deliberate choices, which are often invisible to us.
Human-centered design felt like the missing piece of my intellectual pursuit, as it is a perfect blend of observation, analysis and problem-solving.
And that, my friend, is how I met UX design.

strengths.title: Dealing with Complexity
strengths.description: I ramp up quickly in complex domains and feel comfortable working within data-heavy systems.

strengths.title: System Thinking
strengths.description: I approach problems by getting a bigger picture and uncovering underlying structural challenges.

strengths.title: Tech + AI Fluency
strengths.description: I have a working knowledge of front-end development and design systems. And I'm actively experimenting with, and sharing my learnings about various AI tools in my design workflows.

strengths.title: UX Advocacy
strengths.description: I'm passionate about evangelizing UX design. I've given a presentation with over 60% audience engagement rate during a lunch and learn session.
